fact: November 22 is exactly 31 days before winter solstice in the Northern Hemisphere - that's like a countdown timer to the shortest day of the year!
In Alaska, the sun rises after 9 AM and sets before 4 PM on this date, giving kids less than 7 hours of daylight to play outside.
Squirrels are in full panic mode right now! They're burying up to 25 nuts per day and can remember thousands of hiding spots using landmarks like rocks and trees.
The zodiac sign switches from Scorpio to Sagittarius around November 22, making it one of only 12 days each year when the star signs change over.
Tree sap starts moving super slowly in cold regions around this time - it's like the trees are switching to slow-motion mode for winter!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 22 brings the smell of jasmine and honeysuckle blooming as spring heats up toward summer.
Why do sunsets look extra orange and red in late November? The sun sits lower in the sky, so its light travels through more atmosphere, filtering out blue colors!
Deer are in their mating season right now, which means male deer are showing off by making weird grunting sounds and fighting with their antlers.
November 22 falls in the 47th week of the year - that means we've already lived through 88% of the entire year by this date!
Evergreen trees like pine and spruce are secretly getting ready to survive freezing temperatures by filling their needles with natural antifreeze chemicals.
The moon looks bigger and brighter in late November because cold air holds less water vapor, making the atmosphere clearer for stargazing.
Geese flying south create their famous V-formation around this time, and the lead goose changes every few minutes because flying in front is exhausting!
